model: JackFram/llama-160m (FP, one-shot ternary, per-tensor, PyTorch screen)
finding: BitNet absmean is already near-optimal for per-tensor scale/threshold. MSE-optimal weight reconstruction HURTS CE (weight-MSE != output/CE), lower thresholds (more zeros) hurt (absmean's 0.69 nonzero beats 0.52), and a crude diagonal activation-MSE also hurts. The scale/threshold OBJECTIVE is not the bottleneck -> the bottleneck is the assignment / activation-awareness / inter-layer interaction (RT-124C AWQ/SmoothQuant, then RT-125 GPTQ/Hessian).
